title: phaseolus arrabidae
intro: Phaseolus arrabidae, also known as the Mexican wild bean, is a type of legume plant native to Mexico and Central America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and diarrhea. The health benefits of Phaseolus arrabidae include its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to protect against cell damage and reduce inflammation in the body. In herbal preparations, the seeds and leaves of the plant are often used to make teas, infusions, and poultices, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
